Records.description: Second non-planar two-loop four-point family (called NPB in the paper), a crossed double box in which the massive quark propagates through the left side of the graph and a subset of the right crossed legs while the outer massless legs remain massless. Defined by 9 denominators (7 real propagators plus 2 ISPs). One of the five integral families used to express the two-loop amplitudes for di-jet and photon+jet production via a heavy-quark loop; identities among masters (including the 11 extra triangle-based relations discussed in Sec. 3.2) are derived explicitly for this topology. Canonical basis and epsilon-factorized differential equations given in the Zenodo ancillary, integrated through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$.
Records.familyName: non-planar crossed double box with heavy-quark loop (NPB) / planar double box with heavy-quark loop (PLB)

Records.description: Second planar two-loop four-point family (called PLB in the paper), a planar double box where the middle rung and the right box are traversed by the massive quark while p1 enters a massless corner; the top side and the left vertical are also massive. Defined by 9 denominators (7 real propagators plus 2 ISPs). Part of the five-family set used to express all two-loop amplitudes for q qbar->g gamma, gg->g gamma, q qbar->Q Qbar, q qbar->gg and gg->gg through a heavy-quark loop. Canonical basis and differential equations provided in the Zenodo ancillary; integrated through O(eps^0) in terms of iterated integrals on an elliptic curve.
Records.familyName: planar double box with heavy-quark loop (PLB)

Names: planar double box with heavy-quark loop (PLB)
non-planar crossed double box with heavy-quark loop (NPB)
